stem: In right triangle PQR, the right angle is at Q, PQ = 8, and QR = 15. Point W is any point on side PR. What is the value of cos(angle PQW) − sin(angle WQR)?
rationale: The angles PQW and WQR together make up the right angle PQR, so they are complementary: angle PQW + angle WQR = 90°. For complementary angles, cos of one equals sin of the other: cos(angle PQW) = sin(90° − angle PQW) = sin(angle WQR). Therefore cos(angle PQW) − sin(angle WQR) = 0. The answer does not depend on where W lies on PR.
